rationale: Goal requires proving r1->Some_0 == r2->Some_0 (Vec<u8> SMT spec equality) from only view equality e1@ == r1->Some_0@ and e1@ == r2->Some_0@. In Verus, Vec<T>::view is declared `uninterp spec fn` (see vstd/view.rs:107) and there is no axiom relating spec-level == on Vec to view equality; Vec is not #[verifier::ext_equal] so =~= also fails. Confirmed independently with a 3-line test: `proof fn test(v1: Vec<u8>, v2: Vec<u8>) requires v1@==v2@ ensures v1==v2 {}` fails verification, and =~= likewise fails. group_vec_axioms (axiom_spec_len, axiom_vec_index_decreases, vec_clone_deep_view_proof, axiom_spec_into_iter, axiom_vec_has_resolved, axiom_vec_decreases_to_view) contain no Vec eq axiom; lemma_vec_obeys_view_eq only relates eq_spec (PartialEq) to view, not spec ==. Cannot prove !premise either: when ov is None, both r1 and r2 must be None and the implication still needs equality. Without assume/admit (forbidden), the obligation is unprovable. Generator note in prior attempts: the equali
